description: Korean AI-writing tells. In Korean, the fingerprint of AI-drafted or machine-translated text is 번역투 (translationese): English calques and stiff written-register phrases that a person writing plain Korean would not use. English-only linters cannot see these. Edit this list to fit your house style. The plain-Korean rules here draw on 이오덕's 우리글 바로쓰기 (principles against 일본말 투 and 번역 피동).

rules.hint: Translationese, a calque of 'in the case of'. Use a plain topic marker 은/는, or drop it.
Written/formal connector (the 'therefore' sense). In plain prose use 그래서.
Literary connector. Use 그리고, or start a new sentence.
Calque of 'in terms of / when it comes to'. Usually deletable.
Vague filler ('through / by way of'). Use 로/으로, or name the specific verb.
Hedging calque common in machine-translated news. State it directly.
Academic hedge. Just state the point.
Mechanical AI list-parallelism (첫째·둘째·셋째). Vary the phrasing or use a real list.
이중 피동 (double passive): 되다 is already passive, so ~어지다 doubles it. Use 된다 or 됐다.
Translation-style by-agent passive ('by'). Prefer active voice, or 이/가.
Formal 일본말 투 calque (について). Use 에 대해, or name the specific relation.
Translation-style 'from a date'. Use 부터 directly: 시작일부터 / 그날부터 / 그때부터.
Translationese third-person pronoun. Use the name or 그. Allowlist for fiction if intentional.
